methods.description: This method assesses potential mechanistic effects of toxins using multisequence alignments of EukCat sequences from specific transcriptomes and compares them with mammalian and bacterial sequences to identify potential toxin binding sites.

steps.description: Single-domain EukCat sequences from E. huxleyi and O. sinensis transcriptomes were used.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch EukCat sequences from MMETSP database

steps.description: Multiple sequence alignments of EukCats were performed alongside mammalian and bacterial sequences.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple sequence alignment using MAFFT

steps.description: Sequences were trimmed to visualize potential toxin binding sites and nearby regions.
steps.searchable_summary: trim sequences to focus on toxin binding sites

steps.description: Alignments were visualized with JalView and annotated based on sequence similarity and key residues of interest.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize and annotate alignments using JalView

methods.method_name: Model of Changes in Membrane Potential
methods.description: This method models the potential effects of negative shifts in activation and inactivation voltages due to toxin exposure using electrophysiological characteristics and parameters from a native diatom.

steps.description: Electrophysiological characteristics from the native diatom O. sinensis were used to set model parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: set model parameters using electrophysiological data from O. sinensis

steps.description: A simple model was used to assess effects of negative shifts of V act and V inact due to toxin exposure.
steps.searchable_summary: use model to assess effects of V act and V inact shifts
